mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly employment payrolls consistently reduce equity risk premiums by signaling economic stabilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of months where PAYEMS increases by >50k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% for consecutive months, the zero lower bound environment compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and consumer credit balances signals robust aggregate demand recovery, which expands corporate earnings expectations and lifts equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation when both HOUST and RSAFS rise simultaneously, or joint signal IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points acts as a strong labor market inflection point, reducing recession fears and triggering positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are flat or negative in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops >0.3pp, or predictive R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth above trend, supply chain normalization expands profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings catch up to capacity utilization.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the mechanism fails to predict returns during supply-side bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit growth accelerates while housing starts remain elevated, the combined demand shock outpaces current supply constraints, driving equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not elevated when both RSAFS and HOUST show simultaneous acceleration, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment where CPI remains below 2.5% year-over-year, accommodative monetary policy effectively supports equity risk premiums, generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL stays below 2.5% YoY, or the signal loses predictive power once inflation expectations become unanchored.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% annually signals sustainable corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as fundamentals anchor valuations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 grows >2.0% annually, or the signal produces negative returns during high-valuation regimes.
